factors.name: US Dollar weakness (DXY)
factors.summary: USD retreating sharply across multiple pairs (EUR/USD toward 1.1645, GBP/USD toward 1.3500, AUD/USD at 0.7167) on geopolitical de-escalation optimism. Weaker USD is single largest structural bullish driver for gold.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news; however, expectations for potential Hormuz reopening and oil price collapse ($90–$95 WTI/Brent) suggest inflation risks subsiding. Real yields likely stable to slightly lower, mildly supportive.

factors.name: Oil prices / inflation expectations
factors.summary: Crude oil fell 6–7% on Monday (WTI near $90/bbl, down from >$100) on hopes of Hormuz reopening and 30-day ceasefire extension. Lower energy inflation reduces Fed inflation-fight urgency, easing pressure on real rates.

factors.name: Geopolitical de-escalation (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Trump administration and Iran negotiating ceasefire extension (60 days), Strait reopening in ~30 days, and uranium enrichment compromise. Risk-off pressure lifted; risk-on flows favor commodities and weaken safe-haven USD.

factors.name: Risk sentiment (equities, commodities, carry)
factors.summary: Equity futures surging to all-time highs on Hormuz peace optimism. AUD/USD, commodity-linked currencies rallying. Risk-on environment typically benefits gold via weaker USD more than it hurts gold via safe-haven flows in this scenario.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision in window; however, Bloomberg reports Treasury curve flashing 'higher-for-longer' warning under new Fed chair Warsh. Suggests markets pricing in sustained higher rates, which would normally pressure gold, but is being overwhelmed by USD weakness.

factors.name: Recent gold price action
factors.summary: Gold up ~1.3–1.4% on Monday alone, bouncing from $4,519 lows to $4,570+. Move is fresh but now 3+ hours in; no major intraday pullback yet reported.

factors.name: Underlying geopolitical risk (Israel-Lebanon, Russia-Ukraine)
factors.summary: Despite US-Iran optimism, Israel intensifying strikes on Hezbollah and Lebanon, Russia threatening Kyiv. These cross-currents provide mild safe-haven bid but are being subordinated to Hormuz deal narrative.

factors.name: Gold ETF flows / institutional positioning
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data in news window; positioning unknown. However, intra-day rally suggests some momentum buying into weakness.

factors.name: Competing safe havens
factors.summary: USD weakness implies yen also weakening (USD/JPY retreating from 159.00); JPY strength from intervention fears might compete for safe-haven flows, but net effect is dollar weakness = gold bullish.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied 1.3–1.4% on Monday as hopes for a US-Iran ceasefire extension and Strait of Hormuz reopening (~30 days) collapsed oil prices 6–7% and sent the US Dollar sharply lower across all major pairs. The geopolitical de-escalation narrative is the dominant driver: lower oil implies lower inflation expectations, reducing Fed pressure, while weaker USD mechanically lifts gold. Risk-on sentiment (equities at all-time highs) is reinforcing commodity strength over safe-haven demand. However, the move is now in Phase 2 (underway ~3+ hours), and no pullback has occurred; confidence is capped by the lack of confirmed retest or reversal structure.
catalysts_ahead: US Consumer Confidence (CB) due Tuesday (May 26) — economic strength reading could stabilize USD
Earnings releases Tuesday (Elbit Systems, Zscaler, Semtech) — limited macro impact
Continued US-Iran negotiations (next 24–72h) — any negative headlines would reverse move sharply
Israel-Lebanon escalation risk — if widens, could trigger risk-off and safe-haven gold bid
Fed speakers (Powell, Waller, Williams) — any hawkish surprise could crush gold rally
Actual Hormuz mine-clearing progress / 30-day timeline confirmation — key for oil/inflation narrative
screening.risk_warning: The dominant risk is a reversal in the US-Iran narrative. If negotiations stall, fail, or geopolitical violence escalates (Israel-Lebanon, Houthis, etc.), oil will spike back above $100, inflation expectations reset higher, USD will reverse and strengthen, and gold will face sharp downside. Additionally, any hawkish Fed commentary (Warsh 'higher-for-longer' signals) or surprise inflation print would invalidate the lower-real-yields tailwind. Gold is also entering overbought short-term (up 1.4% in one session on thin volume); late-cycle phases often see quick pullbacks before resuming higher.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress and not yet 4+ hours extended; consider scale-in on a pullback to $4,550–$4,560 (retest of 50% fib of the move) rather than chasing $4,575 resistance. If $4,519 support is broken on escalation news, the entire thesis fails and gold could crater to $4,450–$4,480.

factors.name: USD Strength (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY holding above 99.00 and firming on safe-haven demand amid Iran deal uncertainty. USD/CHF strengthened to 0.7830 snapping 4-day losses; multiple pairs (GBP/USD, EUR/USD, AUD/USD) all weakening. Strong dollar is directly bearish for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: Treasuries rose across the curve on Monday (Memorial Day) as oil crashed ~7% on Iran deal optimism, reducing inflation near-term concerns. However, BOJ Deputy Governor Himino flagged that markets see long-term rate increases reflecting global inflation worries, keeping real yields supported. Mixed but tilt to rate support/inflation risk = mild headwind for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh FOMC or Fed speak in the news window. Market focus on BOJ (rate hike path reaffirmed, Middle East risk caveat). No US inflation data or economic releases in the past 24h to shift Fed expectations materially.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: US launched fresh 'self-defense strikes' on southern Iran (missile sites, boats) on Monday evening, yet simultaneous peace talks in Doha and Trump statement that negotiations are 'proceeding nicely' suggest deal confidence. Market interpretation: strikes + talks = de-escalation path, not widening conflict. Risk-off initially priced, but hope for resolution is reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI plunged ~5-7% on Monday as Iran deal optimism outweighed strikes. Currently trading ~$91.79, down 4.98% per latest report. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations, reducing gold's inflation hedge appeal and supporting USD via lower inflation-driven rate expectations.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Equity futures surged after Iran deal reports; KOSPI hit record high. Risk appetite improving on deal optimism reduces safe-haven flows to gold. AUD/USD, NZD/USD, and other risk-sensitive FX pairs weakening due to USD strength, not equity demand for alternatives.

factors.name: Gold Price Action
factors.summary: Gold slipped below $4,580 during Asian session per FXStreet (03:38 UTC), reversing 'major part' of prior day's move higher. Silver similarly down to ~$76.40. Move underway in bearish direction; multiple sources confirm gold is under selling pressure.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ)
factors.summary: BOJ Deputy Governor Himino reaffirmed rate hike path but tied timing to Middle East developments. No immediate hawkish surprise; JPY carry unwind risk remains structural but not acute. USD/JPY near 159.00 but no breakthrough move yet.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent)
factors.summary: No recent major CPI/PCE release in the news window. Sri Lanka raised policy rate 100bp citing Middle East oil inflation, but this is backward-looking and not a US inflation surprise. No new US inflation print to trigger gold reaction.

factors.name: Macro Confluence
factors.summary: Strong USD + falling oil + peace deal optimism + equity rallies = structural headwinds for gold. Real yields not falling sharply; nominal yields supported. Safe-haven bid is evaporating as geopolitical risk is being priced as containable.

key_levels.resistance: $4,580 (daily resistance, point of reversal), $4,600 (prior swing high)

screening.narrative: Gold is under renewed selling pressure in Asian trade as USD strengthens on safe-haven demand mixed with deal optimism from simultaneous US-Iran peace talks. While fresh US military strikes initially sparked flight-to-safety, the market is interpreting them alongside Trump's 'proceeding nicely' rhetoric and Rubio's confirmation of Qatar talks as a path to ceasefire, not escalation. Oil has crashed ~5-7% on this view, reducing inflation near-term fears and removing the inflation-hedge bid from gold. With DXY holding above 99.00 and risk appetite recovering (equity futures, KOSPI at record), gold's dual bullish factors (safe-haven + inflation hedge) are both fading.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal language finalization (Rubio indicated 'a few days' remaining; could be resolved by 27-28 May)
Bank of Korea rate decision (28 May, expected hold but 70%+ forecast hike by Q3)
Any escalation in US-Iran conflict or breakdown in Qatar talks (would reverse bearish bias sharply)
US economic data (no major release imminent in next 24-48h per news; NFP not until early June)
Oil price stabilization — if Brent/WTI bounce on deal delay, could underpin inflation expectations and gold
BoJ communications / policy shift hint
screening.risk_warning: BIAS FLIP RISK: If Iran deal talks collapse or new military escalation occurs (Israel-Hezbollah, Russia-Ukraine flare-up), or if oil rebounds sharply above $100, gold's safe-haven bid returns immediately and could invalidate bearish setup. Conversely, if real yields drop sharply on Fed dovish pivot or US recession fears, gold could turn bullish regardless of USD strength. Watch for any major US inflation miss or downside NFP ahead of FOMC.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold is in PHASE_2 downtrend (move underway for 3-6h, down ~$15-25 from prior day highs). SELL on retest of $4,580 resistance with risk above $4,600; TARGET $4,550-$4,530 support. For longs, wait for gold to close below $4,550 (potential capitulation spike) or for headlines to flip bearish (deal collapse, oil bounce) before reconsidering BUY.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY clings to gains above 99.00 and near 23.6% Fibonacci support per FXStreet (07:56 UTC). Dollar strength from hawkish Fed risks and geopolitical uncertainty supports USD, pressuring gold inversely.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: 10y Treasury yield fell 6bps to 4.51% on peace-talk optimism (09:19 UTC), but this reflects risk-off technicals into geopolitical chop rather than dovish Fed pivot. No fresh TIPS data, but nominal yield direction suggests real yields holding above zero.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy / Hawkish Guidance
factors.summary: Recent FOMC minutes and Fed Governor Waller comments point to 'higher two-way risk for rates' per BNY (06:54 UTC). ECB chief economist Lane signals June rate hike likely (09:03 UTC), reinforcing hawkish macro backdrop.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tensions (US-Iran Escalation)
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran missile sites and boats (09:44 UTC) cloud peace-deal outlook after initial optimism; Iran downed MQ-9 drone and vowed response per IRGC (07:47 UTC). Tension tug-of-war creates chop; no clear safe-haven bid for gold yet.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI rebounding to ~$93 from overnight $89.40 lows on fresh US-Iran strikes, while Brent at 98.1 USD/bbl per Danske Bank (06:23 UTC). Oil volatility fuels inflation concerns but not yet decisively bullish for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equities
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures +0.54% toward 7,550; Dow futures +0.53% above 50,900 (08:40 UTC). Risk-on tone persists despite geopolitical noise, with investors focusing on peace prospects and spending narratives. Risk appetite competes with gold safe-haven.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / Core Data
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE/PPI releases in news window. ECB inflation concerns cited (09:27 UTC) but no new prints to drive real-yield reset. Lack of fresh inflation data limits directional conviction.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: India gold import hike noted via Muthoot Finance (07:42 UTC), but no headline central bank purchases reported in this news window. Structural bid present but not a near-term catalyst.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows / Positioning
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU holdings data in news. Physical demand remains muted per absence of seasonal headlines (no Indian wedding/festival season traction).

factors.name: Price Action / Technical Momentum
factors.summary: Gold down -0.40% in 4h and -0.27% in 24h, languishing near daily lows per FXStreet (07:49 UTC). Weakness below $4,580 resistance retested; offer tone persists.

screening.narrative: Gold remains under pressure from a toxic mix of hawkish Fed/ECB guidance, firming real yields, and dollar strength driven by geopolitical uncertainty premium on the USD. While US-Iran peace-talk headlines sparked initial relief and 10y yield dip to 4.51%, fresh US military strikes have reignited escalation risk and dampened any safe-haven bid. Equities remain bid on spend-driven narratives, limiting gold's traditional risk-off appeal. The phase-2 selloff is still in motion but lacks extended momentum (only -0.40% in 4h), creating a chop-into-event environment ahead of any major releases or geopolitical resolution.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal framework outcome (ongoing, Qatar talks)
ECB June rate hike decision (likely hawkish per Lane / Schnabel comments)
Fed speakers / Powell comments
Next CPI/PCE print (date TBD, but likely later this week or next week)
NFP / employment data
Oil supply / Strait of Hormuz re-opening timeline
screening.risk_warning: Escalation in US-Iran conflict could trigger sharp risk-off, reversing gold weakness and rallying safe-havens. Conversely, a surprise dovish Fed pivot or major inflation miss would flip the bias bullish. Watch for a formal peace agreement announcement, which would likely relieve USD premium and improve real-yield outlook, pressuring gold further. Chop around $4,500–$4,580 likely until a clearer macro catalyst emerges.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in phase 2 and lacks extended momentum; sell on any retest of $4,550–$4,580 resistance with stops above $4,600. Avoid chasing lower until $4,480 support breaks with volume. Await clarity on US-Iran talks or next inflation print before adding conviction.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (safe-haven bid)
factors.summary: Fresh US-Iran military escalation (US strikes on Iran, Iran vows retaliation) is driving flight-to-safety USD demand. EUR/USD down 0.15% to 1.1622, USD/JPY at new session highs (159.35), inversely pressuring gold. TD Securities notes stagflationary Iran risks but emphasizes higher-for-longer Fed stance supporting USD.

factors.name: Real yields (TIPS / nominal yield direction)
factors.summary: Fed expectations remain hawkish: TD Securities, Citadel Securities both signal Fed should hike or hold higher-for-longer into 2026; June rate hike odds firm per ECB parallels. No fresh TIPS data in news, but hawkish Fed repricing pushes nominal yields higher, likely raising real yields and capping gold upside.

factors.name: Oil price rally (inflation concerns vs. risk-off)
factors.summary: Brent crude jumps above $100/bbl on Iran escalation; oil prices rising support inflationary narrative. However, oil rally is offsetting gold's typical safe-haven move—oil strength favors USD (stagflationary repricing) and competes with gold as inflation hedge. Net effect mixed.

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (Iran-US-Israel tensions)
factors.summary: US strikes on Iran, Iran threatens retaliation, Israel operations in southern Lebanon, Hormuz blockade concerns, US Navy resuming escort missions. Classic safe-haven trigger, but USD safe-haven bid is already priced in and dominating over gold's traditional hedge appeal. Escalation is REAL but gold is losing the race vs. dollar.

factors.name: US Consumer Confidence slippage
factors.summary: Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index fell to 93.1 (vs. 93.8 prior), Present Situation Index down 3.2 pts to 121.2, citing rising prices from Iran war. Softening confidence supports risk-off tone but also validates Fed's hawkish stance (inflation concerns justify hold/higher-for-longer).

factors.name: ECB hawkish repricing (policy divergence with Fed easing cycle)
factors.summary: ECB's Schnabel signals June rate hike (~80% market odds); Bank of France governor says ECB will do what necessary to tame inflation. Stronger EUR prospects (if ECB hikes, EUR resilience) would weaken EUR/USD, strengthen DXY, pressuring gold. Higher-for-longer global yields compete with gold.

factors.name: BoJ gradual tightening (USD/JPY upside continuation)
factors.summary: USD/JPY breaking above 159.337 on BoJ rate hike expectations (Q2 2026 25bp hike, 2.0% terminal rate by end-2027). Rising USD/JPY supports broader USD strength and yen carry unwind, reinforcing dollar bid that pressures gold.

factors.name: Gold technical/price action
factors.summary: XAUUSD down 0.12% in 4h and -0.37% in 24h; FXStreet noted gold slipped from intraday high of $4,580 to $4,525, losing ~$55 on renewed escalation + USD rebound. Move is small but directionally weak; no breakout, consolidating under resistance.

factors.name: Positioning/sentiment shifts
factors.summary: Rabobank notes speculative USD positioning has turned net SHORT for first time since early March as safe-haven flows previously supported dollar. Implication: recent USD strength is not yet fully positioned, leaving room for further dollar upside as Iran risks lock in haven flows.

factors.name: Macro bias history (3 consecutive SELL reads)
factors.summary: May 26 05:02 (SELL 6/10), 09:53 (SELL 6/10), and current lean confirm persistent downside bias. No BUY trigger since May 26 00:04. Sustained negative sentiment from macro analyst.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught between two competing forces: classic safe-haven demand from Iran-US escalation (Strait of Hormuz tensions, military strikes, retaliation threats) and a dominant US Dollar strength bid driven by Fed hawkishness and higher-for-longer rate expectations. The dollar is winning the race—EUR/USD down, USD/JPY rallying to new highs, Fed and ECB both signaling extended tightening cycles—which is atypical for geopolitical spikes. Oil's surge above $100 Brent reinforces stagflationary risks that validate the Fed's hawkish hold, pushing real yields higher and capping gold upside. Consumer confidence slipped on inflation, but this only hardens the Fed's resolve to keep rates high. Gold has retreated from $4,580 to $4,509 as the USD rally accelerated post-escalation.
catalysts_ahead: Trump Cabinet meeting at Camp David (Wed 2026-05-27) — potential Iran policy signals or geopolitical updates
ECB June rate decision (early June, not immediate but near) — if confirmed 25bp hike, locks in higher EUR and higher global yields, negative for gold
FOMC minutes or Fed speakers (if scheduled in next 72h) — any hawkish reiteration could cement USD strength
Iran nuclear deal developments / ceasefire negotiations outcome — if deal done, geopolitical safe-haven premium collapses sharply
Oil price action (Brent above $100) — key inflation input; if oil reverses hard on de-escalation, could trigger gold bounce via lower real yields
US economic data (if scheduled): Jobless claims, any preliminary GDP/sentiment reads — softness could ease Fed hawkishness; strength confirms higher-for-longer
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: If Iran-US tensions abruptly de-escalate (ceasefire agreement, Trump deal announcement) and/or if Fed dovish surprise (Citadel/TD analysis proved wrong, Fed signals easing pivot in June), USD could weaken sharply and gold could spike through $4,550–$4,600. Also watch for Oil collapse (if geopolitical risk off-switch flips)—this would reverse stagflation premium, lower real yields, and be BULLISH gold. Conversely, gold is at risk if USD continues breakout and real yields accelerate higher on Fed hawkishness lock-in.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (small decline underway, $4,525 from $4,580 intraday high but <1% from open). Wait for retest of resistance at $4,530–$4,545 before shorting; alternatively, if geopolitical escalation sudden reverses (peace deal headlines), watch for breakdown below $4,490 support before re-engaging longs. Confidence is only 5/10 due to competing narratives (geopolitical bullish, Fed/USD bearish) and lack of major data catalysts in immediate next 6-12h.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) Strength
factors.summary: USD rallying on haven demand after US strikes in southern Iran (FXStreet 18:44). USD/JPY broke above 159.00 resistance, GBP/USD down 0.43%, EUR/USD down 0.15% — broad dollar strength inversely pressures gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Hawkish Pivot Risk
factors.summary: Fed's Kashkari stated Fed 'could embark on a series of rate hikes' if inflation from Middle East turmoil persists (Investinglive 19:14). This hawkish tone, combined with higher-for-longer USD stance (TD Securities), is headwind for real yields being attractive for gold.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike (Geopolitical)
factors.summary: Brent jumped 3%+ after Iran vows retaliation; Strait of Hormuz tensions elevated (multiple sources 19:09–18:54). Higher oil typically feeds inflation narrative and supports Fed hawkishness, which is gold-negative despite safe-haven bid in commodities.

factors.name: Geopolitical Safe-Haven (Initial)
factors.summary: US strikes on Iran, Netanyahu escalation in Lebanon, Hormuz closure risk — traditional safe-haven flows. However, gold already tumbled 1.60% to below $4,500 per FXStreet (18:44), suggesting market has already priced initial fear.

factors.name: Gold Price Action / Recent Move
factors.summary: XAU/USD down 1.60% on the day, 4h change -0.11%, 24h change -0.57%. Reached daily high of $4,580 but retreated sharply. Move already underway suggests late entry risk if chasing downside.

factors.name: Equity Market Resilience & Risk-On Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500, Nasdaq rising on AI optimism (Micron up 20.8% today, +860% YoY, hit $1T market cap); SpaceX IPO euphoria driving space stocks. Risk-on sentiment competes with safe-haven flows and pressures gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / Fed Rate Hike Bets
factors.summary: Kashkari's hawkish comments and higher oil prices reinforce stagflation risk and higher-for-longer rates narrative. July hike bets creeping higher per Dow Jones futures (FXStreet 16:06). Citadel Securities says Fed must shift to inflation fighting (Bloomberg 16:05).

factors.name: US Treasury Auction & Yields
factors.summary: 2Y auction at 4.071% with benign bid-to-cover (2.64x); auction grade C, congruent with 6m average per Investinglive (17:06). US yields reported down on the day overall, but this is pre-fresh supply digest.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying / ETF Flows
factors.summary: No new data on PBoC, RBI, or major ETF flows (GLD/IAU) in news window. Structural bid factors inactive in current cycle.

factors.name: BoJ / USD/JPY Intervention Risk
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 159.38, buyers pushing through 159.00 despite intervention fears (FXStreet 18:54). BoJ unlikely to defend aggressively near key level, supporting yen carry unwind but prolonging USD strength vs gold.

screening.narrative: Gold has been sold off sharply (-1.60% intraday) despite acute Middle East geopolitical risk (US strikes on Iran, Hormuz tensions, Netanyahu escalation) because the dominant driver is US Dollar strength on renewed safe-haven demand and a hawkish Fed pivot narrative. Kashkari's comments on potential rate hike cycles, combined with elevated oil prices feeding inflation concerns, have convinced markets that real yields will stay higher for longer—the primary headwind for gold. Equity market resilience (S&P 500, Nasdaq rallying on AI euphoria) further dampens traditional safe-haven flows into gold, favoring the dollar instead.
catalysts_ahead: Australia CPI release (timing TBD) — inflation surprise could accelerate AUD weakness and amplify USD strength
Trump-Iran ceasefire negotiations (Camp David meeting Wed per WhiteHouse) — escalation/de-escalation will swing geopolitical premium
Potential further Fed hawkish rhetoric or inflation data in coming days
Strait of Hormuz shipping flows — continued tension supports oil, which may eventually tempt gold but so far supports inflation/rate story
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks collapse and Strait of Hormuz closes for extended period, oil could spike to $150+/bbl, triggering acute safe-haven bid in gold despite hawkish yields. Conversely, if ceasefire holds, geopolitical premium evaporates completely and gold could fall another 2-3%. Equity volatility spike (VIX >25) would flip gold back to safe-haven mode. Monitor Fed speakers for any dovish surprises that would lower real-yield pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already underway (-1.6% on the day, gold at fresh lows). Wait for retest of $4,520–$4,550 resistance band before considering new short entries; chasing below $4,500 is late-phase and risks pullback trap into Hormuz escalation headlines. If geopolitical fear re-ignites (Iran retaliation statement or Hormuz closure confirmed), re-enter bullish bias at $4,480 support with tight stop.

factors.name: USD Strength (DXY)
factors.summary: FXStreet reports USD regaining composure on Tuesday amid geopolitical uncertainty and renewed haven demand following US strikes in southern Iran. Strong dollar directly pressures gold; USD/JPY topped 159.38, showing yen weakness and dollar bid.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in today's news, but bond market commentary mentions 'violent selloff' (MarketWatch) and higher real rate regime emerging. Elevated real rates remain structurally headwind for gold, though specific 10y TIPS yield not disclosed.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Fed Stance
factors.summary: Fed's Kashkari (Minneapolis) told Nikkei the Fed 'could embark on a series of hikes in response to inflation' if Middle East conflict keeps pushing oil/inflation higher. This hawkish tone supports USD and weighs on gold despite geopolitical risk.

factors.name: Oil & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Brent crude jumped >3% after Iran vowed retaliation for US strikes (Al Jazeera, 19:09); Piper Sandler forecasts Strait of Hormuz closure for months, sending oil to new highs. Rising energy inflation typically supports gold as safe-haven and inflation hedge.

factors.name: ECB Policy Signals
factors.summary: Bank of France Governor Villeroy de Galhau (CNBC, 20:58) vowed ECB will 'do whatever it takes' to contain inflation; BNY reports ECB hawks eyeing June rate hike due to Middle East energy shocks. Tighter global monetary policy headwind for gold.

factors.name: BoJ & JPY Carry Trade Risk
factors.summary: Stephen Jen (Eurizon SLJ Capital) expects yen to strengthen, threatening carry trades reliant on weak yen. BoJ Governor Ueda speaking today (per calendar note). Yen strength would support safe-haven flows but complicate USD carry unwind.

factors.name: Equity Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Russell 2000 all closed at record highs on May 26 (FXStreet, 20:15); Dow slightly down. Risk-on mood reduces safe-haven demand for gold; equity record highs typically compete with gold allocations.

factors.name: Gold ETF/Positioning
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports options volumes leaned bullish in GLD and GDX on Tuesday, suggesting some institutional interest, but no major flows data disclosed; FXStreet notes gold 'dives' >1.60% on Tuesday.

factors.name: Technical/Price Action
factors.summary: Gold tumbled >1.60% on Tuesday, reaching as low as below $4,500 after daily high of $4,580 (FXStreet, 18:44). Current +0.39% 4h gain and -0.42% 24h loss suggests move already in progress downward; recovery modest.

factors.name: Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Prior three reads all SELL bias (6/10, 5/10, 6/10) with no confirmation triggered. Today's move lower aligns with prior bearish lean but confidence remains low due to lack of triggering confirmation and mixed macro backdrop.

screening.narrative: Gold fell >1.60% on Tuesday as the US Dollar surged on renewed geopolitical risk (fresh US strikes in Iran, Iran retaliatory threats) and hawkish Fed rhetoric (Kashkari's potential rate-hike signal). Although elevated oil prices (+3% Brent on Iran tensions) and energy inflation support gold's inflation-hedge narrative, a stronger USD, rising real yields, and record-high equity prices are outweighing safe-haven demand. The ECB's hawkish positioning and global rate-hiking bias add structural headwind. Prior bias history shows consistent SELL leans without confirmation, and today's move represents Phase 2 extension downward rather than a fresh catalyst.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ Governor Ueda speech (today/overnight, 27 May APAC session)
Australia CPI (Wednesday 27 May, 01:30 GMT) — key inflation data
RBNZ Rate Decision (Wednesday 27 May, APAC session)
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh first full week in office; Powell/Williams/Waller speeches possible
FOMC meeting minutes or next policy decision (dates not explicitly in news)
US Treasury bond auctions (2Y note just completed at 4.071%, ongoing)
Straits of Hormuz/Middle East ceasefire outcome (binary geopolitical risk)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran ceasefire is confirmed and oil prices roll over, or if Fed pivots dovish on inflation worries, gold could re-test resistance $4,550+. Additionally, major BoJ or ECB dovish surprise could weaken USD and support gold. Equity market weakness (VIX spike) would also favor gold safe-haven. Current move is Phase 2 (in progress downward); early support bounce to $4,500–$4,520 should be monitored before confirming further downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway lower (Phase 2, -1.6% from highs); wait for retest or pullback to $4,530–$4,550 resistance before shorting again. If break below $4,500 holds and closes below that level, short bias gains weight; otherwise, chop into upcoming APAC data and BoJ commentary.
